news.title: Alexis Mac Allister says ‘two teams who play good football’ have reached World Cup final
news.lede: Alexis Mac Allister believes Argentina’s hard-fought 2-1 World Cup semifinal victory over England was about more than just reaching another final. Speaking after the match, the midfielder reflected on the direction the modern game is taking and why he feels this year’s finalists represent something special. “Football is becoming more and more physical…and that’s why it’s important that two teams who play good football have reached the final,” Mac Allister said (as captured by transfer journalist Fabrizio Romano). Mac Allister’s comments highlight a growing debate in today’s game.

news.title: World Cup 2026 final fixture and semi-final results
news.lede: This content is provided by Exco Player and may use cookies or similar technologies. Please click 'Allow and Continue' below to load the content. For the first time in the tournament’s history, the top four ranked teams in the world made up the final four. World champions and no2-ranked Argentina took on no4-ranked England, after no1-ranked France faced off against no3-ranked Spain.

news.title: Keir Starmer wants Fifa investigation into Argentina players who held Falklands banner
news.lede: Spokesperson says islands belong to the UK. ‘PM wishes both teams well for the final, especially Spain’. Keir Starmer supports the idea of Fifa investigating Argentina players who displayed a banner touting their country’s claim to the Falklands Islands after their World Cup semi-final win against England, Downing Street has said. Starmer, who watched the match while travelling to Ukraine by train for the final overseas trip of his premiership, endorsed a call by Peter Kyle, the business secretary, for Fifa to investigate what rules may have been broken.

news.title: Here's what 2026 World Cup says about football in Africa
news.lede: To view this video please enable JavaScript, and consider upgrading to a web browser that supports HTML5 video. Nine African teams reached the knockout stages in 2026, outperforming European and Latin American sides in progression rates. The question is not whether Africa belongs here, but how it turns this progress into a title. Nine of Africa's 10 teams reached the knockout stage of the men's World Cup , giving African sides a better progression rate than those from Europe and South America.

news.title: Sports Daily Live: World Cup Final set; college football season getting closer
news.lede: (KWCH) - The World Cup final is set. How excited are American fans for Spain and Argentina? In the world of golf, The Open Championship is underway. What can we expect from the sport’s original major?

news.title: Thursday Flakes: World Cup Final Set, The Open Begins and More
news.lede: Argentina Semifinal match in the World Cup yesterday was absolutely demoralizing. I like England, don’t particularly care for plenty of Argentina’s team, and am “meh” on how I feel about Messi. The one thing I couldn’t stand with England was how they went about that match. The saying goes “If you don’t learn from history, you are doomed to repeat it.”

news.title: Lionel Messi’s heroic run about to face World Cup’s toughest test in final
news.lede: Our World Cup final matchup for Sunday at MetLife Stadium is set — Argentina advanced to play Spain following a dramatic 2-1 win over England in the semifinals on Wednesday. The game in Atlanta provided all-time moments from Enzo Fernandez and Lautaro Martinez — both goals were assisted by Lionel Messi — as Argentina kept its bid alive to win a second consecutive World Cup. And it begged questions of England manager Thomas Tuchel, who put his team in a perilous defensive posture with the timing and choices of his substitutions.

news.title: Which FIFA World Cup semifinalist will return to the semis in 2030?
news.lede: Now that the World Cup final is set, let us start looking forward to 2030. We will look at a few factors for each team that will contribute to their ability to make a deep run in consecutive World Cups. Here are the rankings from least likely to make it to the semifinals again to the most: 4. Argentina Argentina has followed up their 2022 win with another trip to the final.
